title: Agrostis aequivalvis
intro: Agrostis aequivalvis, also known as bent grass, is a type of grass native to the Andes mountains, belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Agrostis aequivalvis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Agrostis aequivalvis is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to soothe skin irritations.
